title: [codex] fix: pass text modalities to providers
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes Pollinations-side image modality rejection from the text Portkey path.
- Leaves registry modality metadata available for catalog/UI use.
- Keeps provider-specific image URL fetch safety in the base64 transform.

Checks:
- `npx biome check --write gen.pollinations.ai/src/text/generateTextPortkey.ts`
- `npm run test --prefix gen.pollinations.ai -- test/text/transforms/imageUrlToBase64Transform.test.ts`
- `npm run typecheck --prefix gen.pollinations.ai`
- `git diff --check`

Fixes #12025

gist.headline: Opening the multimodal gates
gist.blurb: We've removed our strict modality bouncers from the text API. Your multimodal requests now flow straight through to the providers, just as nature intended.
gist.summary: Removed Pollinations-side image modality validation from the text Portkey path, allowing multimodal requests to pass directly to providers.
gist.impact: Developers can now send multimodal inputs to supported text models without encountering premature validation errors from our gateway.
gist.visual_concept: The bee (Polly) opening a gate or removing a barrier for different types of data (text documents, image files) to flow freely into a glowing portal (Portkey/provider). Nomnom creature happily watching the data flow.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot opening a small wooden toll gate, allowing a stream of glowing text documents and pixelated image files to flow freely into a glowing portal. The round tan nomnom creature sits nearby, happily watching the data flow. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
